zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de la Hyde於大約1230年出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾加索普時，她的父親Lord Robert de la Hyde39歲，母親Lady Agnes de Herdislee35歲。她於大約1245年10月與Sir Bewes L de Knoville在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1276年在英國史洛普爾其其享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de la Hyde于大约1230年出生在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾加索普时，她的父亲Lord Robert de la Hyde39岁，母亲Lady Agnes de Herdislee35岁。她于大约1245年10月与Sir Bewes L de Knoville在英国戴文结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1276年在英国史洛普爾其其享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Hyde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of various places so called in Bedfordshire, Cheshire, Hampshire, and Middlesex. They were named with Old English hīd ‘hide (of land)’, a variable measure of land, differing from place to place and time to time, and seems to have been originally fixed as the amount necessary to support one (extended) family (Old English hīgan, hīwan ‘household’). The surname may also be topographic for someone living on (and farming) a hide of land. The Hyde family has been in Leinster, Ireland, since the early 13th century and one family has been established in the county of Cork since the 16th century. The name was Gaelicized as both Dalaithíd and de hÍde. Compare Hyder .

variants.name: Dollarhide
meanings: Irish: possibly an altered form of de la Hyde (see Hyde ), which was a prominent surname in Leinster from medieval times onward. Compare Dollahite .

History: Francis Dollahide, born c. 1650–60 in County Dublin, Ireland, was the ancestor of Americans with the surname Dollahide, Dollahite, and Dollarhide. In 1680 he was transported to MD for some youthful misdemeanor. He appears to have been an educated man and rose to be an important landowner and politician, serving in the Lower House of the MD legislature as an elected representative for Baltimore County from c. 1705 to 1721.

variants.name: Hyder
meanings: English (Kent and Sussex): status name or topographic name for one who lived on or possessed a hide of land, from Middle English hide + the locative suffix -er. This is a characteristically southeastern English name, synonymous with Middle English atte hide elsewhere (see Hyde ).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de la Hyde was born about 1230, in Garthorpe, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Lord Robert de la Hyde, was 39 and her mother, Lady Agnes de Herdislee, was 35. She married Sir Bewes L de Knoville about October 1245, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died about 1276, in Whitchurch, Shropshire, England, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de la Hyde est née environ 1230, à Garthorpe, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Lord Robert de la Hyde, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes de Herdislee, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Bewes L de Knoville environ octobre 1245, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1276, à Whitchurch, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de la Hyde um 1230 in Garthorpe, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lord Robert de la Hyde 39 und ihre Mutter Lady Agnes de Herdislee 35. Sie hat um Oktober 1245 in Devon, England Sir Bewes L de Knoville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1276 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Whitchurch, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de la Hyde è nata il circa 1230 nel Garthorpe, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Lord Robert de la Hyde, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Lady Agnes de Herdislee, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Sir Bewes L de Knoville circa ottobre 1245, nel Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1276, nel Whitchurch, Shropshire, England, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de la Hydeが約1230年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアガーソルペで生まれた時，父Lord Robert de la Hydeは39歳，母Lady Agnes de Herdisleeは35歳であった。約1245年10月にイギリスデボンでSir Bewes L de Knovilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1276年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアウィトチャーチで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de la Hyde(이)가 대략 1230년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 가소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord Robert de la Hyde(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Agnes de Herdislee(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 대략 1245년 10월 영국 데폰에서 Sir Bewes L de Knoville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1276년 영국 슈럽실르 휘트c후르치에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de la Hyde nasceu aproximadamente 1230, em Garthorpe, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Lord Robert de la Hyde, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Lady Agnes de Herdislee, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Bewes L de Knoville aproximadamente outubro de 1245, em Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1276, em Whitchurch, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de la Hyde родилась около 1230 г. в местности гартаурп, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Lord Robert de la Hyde было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes de Herdislee, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Bewes L de Knoville около октябрь 1245 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1276 г. в местности уичурч, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de la Hyde nació aproximadamente 1230, en Garthorpe, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Lord Robert de la Hyde, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Lady Agnes de Herdislee, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Sir Bewes L de Knoville aproximadamente octubre de 1245, en Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1276, en Whitchurch, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 48 años.
